Figure 2:Calibration verification (Theorem 1).Left:ECE
for individual evidence (0.1400.140), LPF-SPN (0.1850.185), and
LPF-Learned (0.0580.058), with Hoeffding (0.7720.772) and
Bernstein (0.4590.459) tight bounds annotated.Centre and right:reliability diagrams for LPF-SPN
and LPF-Learned showing confidence vs. accuracy against the
perfect-calibration diagonal.

Figure 3:Monte Carlo error bounds (Theorem 2).Left:log-log plot of mean error, 95th-percentile error, and
theoretical bound vs.M∈{4,8,16,32,64}M\in\{4,8,16,32,64\}; all
empirical curves remain well below the bound.Right:normalised error scaling confirms the
empirical rate closely tracksO​(1/M)O(1/\sqrt{M})theory.

Figure 4:Generalization bound verification (Theorem 3).Top-left:train and test loss learning curves with
confidence intervals acrossN∈{2002,3003,4200}N\in\{2002,3003,4200\}.Top-right:empirical gap (near zero) vs. VC bound
(loose) and data-dependent PAC-Bayes bound (tight,0.2280.228atN=4200N=4200).Bottom-left:bound-to-gap ratio on
a log scale.Bottom-right:test loss vs.NNwith effective dimensiondeff=1335d_{\mathrm{eff}}=1335marked.

Figure 5:Information-theoretic lower bound (Theorem 4).Top-left:decomposition of total uncertaintyH​(Y)=1.399H(Y)=1.399bits into evidence informationI​(E;Y)=1.399I(E;Y)=1.399and residualH​(Y|E)≈0H(Y|E)\approx 0.Top-right:ECE comparison — theoretical lower
bound (0.1580.158), achievable bound including MC term
(0.3170.317), and LPF-SPN empirical ECE (0.1780.178).Bottom-left:evidence quality distribution

Figure 6:Robustness to evidence corruption (Theorem 5).Left:empirical L1 distance‖pclean−pcorrupted‖\|p_{\text{clean}}-p_{\text{corrupted}}\|(blue) remains near zero while the
theoreticalO​(ϵ​K)O(\epsilon\sqrt{K})bound (red dashed)
grows linearly; the safe region is shaded.Right:bound-to-empirical ratio (up to6×1076\times 10^{7}atϵ=0.1\epsilon=0.1), confirming the bound is highly
conservative in practice.

Figure 7:Sample complexity scaling (Theorem 6).Top-left:LPF-Learned ECE (blue) and baseline
uniform ECE (green) both lie far below the theoreticalO​(1/K)O(1/\sqrt{K})bound (red dashed) forK∈{1,…,20}K\in\{1,\ldots,20\}.Top-right:bound-to-empirical ECE ratio.Bottom-left:O​(1/K)O(1/\sqrt{K})fit
(0.25/K+0.120.25/\sqrt{K}+0.12,R2=0.849R^{2}=0.849) with empirical ECE
plateauing atK≈7K\approx 7.Bottom-right:LPF vs. uniform baseline atK∈{1,2,3,5}K\in\{1,2,3,5\}; baseline available only forK≥5K\geq 5.

Figure 8:Uncertainty decomposition (Theorem 7).Top-left:total, epistemic (reducible), and
aleatoric (irreducible) variance vs.KK, showing the
non-monotonic epistemic trajectory (risesK=1→3K=1\to 3, fallsK=3→5K=3\to 5) while aleatoric variance stabilises at≈0.042\approx 0.042.Top-right:stacked area chart of variance
components.Bottom-left:decomposition error remains<0.002%<0.002\%, well below the10%10\%threshold (dashed).Bottom-right:epistemic variance isolated,
confirming reduction with additional evidence against the
constant aleatoric floor (≈0.020\approx 0.020).
